shared_facts: A Paris appeals court upheld Marine Le Pen's conviction regarding the misuse of European Parliament funds.
The court reduced the duration of the ban on Le Pen holding public office.
The court ordered that Le Pen must wear an electronic monitoring bracelet.
Le Pen has expressed reservations about running for president if she is required to wear an electronic monitoring device.
latest_clusters.synthesis: A Paris appeals court has upheld Marine Le Pen's conviction for misuse of European Parliament funds while modifying her sentence to include an electronic monitoring tag. The ruling reduced her ban on holding public office, leaving her formal eligibility to run in the 2027 presidential election open. Le Pen must now determine if she will proceed with a presidential bid given the conditions of her sentence.
latest_clusters.confidence: MEDIUM. While all sources agree on the core outcome—a conviction and the requirement of an electronic tag—there are discrepancies in the specific legal definitions of the sentence length and the structure of the ban.

latest_impact.title: Cuba power blackouts
latest_impact.why_it_matters: Cuba experienced a total nationwide power outage on Monday, marking the third such event this year. State-run utility officials have initiated an investigation into the cause while working to restore services to critical sectors. The country continues to face a significant energy crisis fueled by chronic fuel shortages and aging infrastructure.
